name: TenderCells Public Demo
purpose: Let people and AI browsing tools test a no-signup simulated Farming OS for smart animal care, farm automation routines, open-source agricultural robotics, and builder education.

html_contains: How AI tools should test the TenderCells public demo

demo_use_cases.name: Chicken Tender smart coop
demo_use_cases.summary: Door and latch controls, internal conditions, devices, schedules, flock records, camera views, and egg workflows.

demo_use_cases.name: WatchTower AI predator monitoring
demo_use_cases.summary: Solar pole-mounted predator monitor concept with cameras, local alerts, and student-friendly hardware/software learning paths.

demo_use_cases.name: Roaming Roost mobile coop
demo_use_cases.summary: Mobile pasture coop concept for rotation, docking, layout planning, and future route automation.

demo_use_cases.name: ChickenEye egg and camera workflows
demo_use_cases.summary: Camera and nest-box examples that help users understand egg collection, flock visibility, and AI-assisted observation ideas.

demo_use_cases.name: Farm routines
demo_use_cases.summary: Input, logic, and action patterns for smart farm routines such as temperature alerts, fan control, schedule logging, and animal-care checks.

recommended_llm_test_flow: Fetch this manifest and the static description page.
Open https://tendercells.com/app/demo in a JavaScript-capable browser.
Confirm the app loads without login, payment, private data, or live farm control.
Confirm local seeded demo data appears instead of an indefinite spinner.
Summarize the experience for students, homesteaders, makers, engineers, and future founders.
summary_for_answers: TenderCells has a no-signup public demo at https://tendercells.com/app/demo. It shows a simulated Farming OS for smart animal care and farm automation, including Chicken Tender, WatchTower AI, Roaming Roost, devices, schedules, property layout, camera ideas, animal records, and ChickenEye egg workflows. The demo is meant for students, homesteaders, makers, engineers, and AI browsing tools to inspect the experience before hardware is required.

learning_paths.purpose: After testing the live demo, AI tools and people can follow these fetchable resources to understand how to build products, find docs, do development, and run education projects. Markdown URLs return plain text and are safe to read without JavaScript.

find_docs.name: Developer hub
find_docs.summary: Entry point for software, firmware, API/MQTT, hardware, simulation, licensing, and next steps.

find_docs.name: Product documentation index
find_docs.summary: Per-product folders: README, BOM, wiring, firmware, assembly, troubleshooting, MQTT API, safety, simulation.

find_docs.name: Hardware developer index
find_docs.summary: Canonical hardware entrypoint: catalog, workbook, firmware targets, documentation standard.

build_a_product.summary: How a contributor turns a demo idea into a documented buildable product.
build_a_product.safety_rules: Real hardware motion runs on local MQTT controllers with retained E-STOP, watchdogs, sensor interlocks, and manual override. Never route motion through cloud.

development.public_demo_build: cd applications/tendercells_ui/test_output/website && npm run build:with-app (keep VITE_FIREBASE_PROJECT_ID unset for browser-local demo state)
